  have h₁ : (30 * y) % 47 = 42 % 47 := h
  have h₂ : y ≥ 39 := by
    by_contra h₃
    -- If y < 39, then y ≤ 38
    have h₄ : y ≤ 38 := by linarith
    interval_cases y <;> norm_num [Nat.mul_mod, Nat.mod_mod] at h₁ ⊢ <;>
      (try omega) <;> (try norm_num at h₁ ⊢) <;> (try omega)
  exact h₂

    rw [Nat.ModEq] at hy
    norm_num at hy ⊢
    -- Use the fact that 30 * y % 47 = 42 % 47 to find the smallest y
    have h : y ≥ 39 := by
      by_contra h₁
      -- If y < 39, then y ≤ 38
      have h₂ : y ≤ 38 := by linarith
      -- Check all possible values of y from 0 to 38 to find a contradiction
      interval_cases y <;> norm_num [Nat.mul_mod, Nat.add_mod] at hy ⊢ <;>
        (try omega) <;> (try contradiction)
    linarith

simpa using h₄

      have h₇ : x ≤ 38 := by linarith
      interval_cases x <;> norm_num at h₅ ⊢ <;>
        (try omega) <;> (try contradiction) <;> (try norm_num at h₅ ⊢ <;> omega)

smallest x
    have h₃ : (30 * x) % 47 = 42 % 47 := by

      have h₄ : x ≤ 38 := by

have h₀ : 39 ∈ { x : ℕ | 30 * x ≡ 42 [MOD 47] } := by
    norm_num [Nat.ModEq, Nat.mod_eq_of_lt]
    <;> rfl
  have h₁ : ∀ (x : ℕ), x ∈ { x : ℕ | 30 * x ≡ 42 [MOD 47] } → 39 ≤ x := by
    intro x hx
    rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hx
    have h₂ : 30 * x % 47 = 42 % 47 := by
      rw [Nat.ModEq] at hx
      exact hx
    have h₃ : x ≥ 39 := by
      by_contra h
      have h₄ : x ≤ 38 := by linarith
      interval_cases x <;> norm_num [Nat.mul_mod, Nat.mod_mod] at h₂ ⊢ <;>
        (try omega) <;> (try contradiction)
    linarith
  exact ⟨h₀, h₁⟩
